dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOpenClaw\u003c/strong\u003e is a \u003cem\u003epersonal AI assistant\u003c/em\u003e you run on your own devices.\nIt answers you on the channels you already use (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord, Google Chat, Signal, iMessage, Microsoft Teams, WebChat), plus extension channels like BlueBubbles, Matrix, Zalo, and Zalo Personal. It can speak and listen on macOS/iOS/Android, and can render a live Canvas you control. The Gateway is just the control plane — the product is the assistant.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you want a personal, single-user assistant that feels local, fast, and always-on, this is it.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://openclaw.ai\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eWebsite\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eDocs\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md\"\u003eVision\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://deepwiki.com/openclaw/openclaw\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eDeepWiki\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/getting-started\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eGetting Started\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/install/updating\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eUpdating\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/showcase\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eShowcase\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/faq\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eFAQ\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/wizard\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eWizard\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/openclaw/nix-openclaw\"\u003eNix\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/install/docker\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eDocker\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://discord.gg/clawd\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eDiscord\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePreferred setup: run the onboarding wizard (\u003ccode\u003eopenclaw onboard\u003c/code\u003e) in your terminal.\nThe wizard guides you step by step through setting up the gateway, workspace, channels, and skills. The CLI wizard is the recommended path and works on \u003cstrong\u003emacOS, Linux, and Windows (via WSL2; strongly recommended)\u003c/strong\u003e.\nWorks with npm, pnpm, or bun.\nNew install? Treat inbound DMs as \u003cstrong\u003euntrusted input\u003c/strong\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFull security guide: \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/security\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eSecurity\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDefault behavior on Telegram/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/Microsoft Teams/Discord/Google Chat/Slack:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDM pairing\u003c/strong\u003e (\u003ccode\u003edmPolicy=\"pairing\"\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003echannels.discord.dmPolicy=\"pairing\"\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003echannels.slack.dmPolicy=\"pairing\"\u003c/code\u003e; legacy: \u003ccode\u003echannels.discord.dm.policy\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003echannels.slack.dm.policy\u003c/code\u003e): unknown senders receive a short pairing code and the bot does not process their message.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eApprove with: \u003ccode\u003eopenclaw pairing approve \u0026lt;channel\u0026gt; \u0026lt;code\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e (then the sender is added to a local allowlist store).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003ePublic inbound DMs require an explicit opt-in: set \u003ccode\u003edmPolicy=\"open\"\u003c/code\u003e and include \u003ccode\u003e\"*\"\u003c/code\u003e in the channel allowlist (\u003ccode\u003eallowFrom\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003echannels.discord.allowFrom\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003echannels.slack.allowFrom\u003c/code\u003e; legacy: \u003ccode\u003echannels.discord.dm.allowFrom\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003echannels.slack.dm.allowFrom\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eRun \u003ccode\u003eopenclaw doctor\u003c/code\u003e to surface risky/misconfigured DM policies.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOpenClaw can auto-configure Tailscale \u003cstrong\u003eServe\u003c/strong\u003e (tailnet-only) or \u003cstrong\u003eFunnel\u003c/strong\u003e (public) while the Gateway stays bound to loopback. Configure \u003ccode\u003egateway.tailscale.mode\u003c/code\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eoff\u003c/code\u003e: no Tailscale automation (default).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003eserve\u003c/code\u003e: tailnet-only HTTPS via \u003ccode\u003etailscale serve\u003c/code\u003e (uses Tailscale identity headers by default).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003efunnel\u003c/code\u003e: public HTTPS via \u003ccode\u003etailscale funnel\u003c/code\u003e (requires shared password auth).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eNotes:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003egateway.bind\u003c/code\u003e must stay \u003ccode\u003eloopback\u003c/code\u003e when Serve/Funnel is enabled (OpenClaw enforces this).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eServe can be forced to require a password by setting \u003ccode\u003egateway.auth.mode: \"password\"\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003egateway.auth.allowTailscale: false\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eFunnel refuses to start unless \u003ccode\u003egateway.auth.mode: \"password\"\u003c/code\u003e is set.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eOptional: \u003ccode\u003egateway.tailscale.resetOnExit\u003c/code\u003e to undo Serve/Funnel on shutdown.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDetails: \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/tailscale\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eTailscale guide\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/web\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eWeb surfaces\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eRemote Gateway (Linux is great)\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-remote-gateway-linux-is-great\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Remote Gateway (Linux is great)\" href=\"#remote-gateway-linux-is-great\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIt’s perfectly fine to run the Gateway on a small Linux instance. Clients (macOS app, CLI, WebChat) can connect over \u003cstrong\u003eTailscale Serve/Funnel\u003c/strong\u003e or \u003cstrong\u003eSSH tunnels\u003c/strong\u003e, and you can still pair device nodes (macOS/iOS/Android) to execute device‑local actions when needed.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGateway host\u003c/strong\u003e runs the exec tool and channel connections by default.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDevice nodes\u003c/strong\u003e run device‑local actions (\u003ccode\u003esystem.run\u003c/code\u003e, camera, screen recording, notifications) via \u003ccode\u003enode.invoke\u003c/code\u003e.\nIn short: exec runs where the Gateway lives; device actions run where the device lives.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDetails: \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/remote\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eRemote access\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eNodes\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/security\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eSecurity\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003emacOS permissions via the Gateway protocol\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-macos-permissions-via-the-gateway-protocol\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: macOS permissions via the Gateway protocol\" href=\"#macos-permissions-via-the-gateway-protocol\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe macOS app can run in \u003cstrong\u003enode mode\u003c/strong\u003e and advertises its capabilities + permission map over the Gateway WebSocket (\u003ccode\u003enode.list\u003c/code\u003e / \u003ccode\u003enode.describe\u003c/code\u003e). Clients can then execute local actions via \u003ccode\u003enode.invoke\u003c/code\u003e:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003esystem.run\u003c/code\u003e runs a local command and returns stdout/stderr/exit code; set \u003ccode\u003eneedsScreenRecording: true\u003c/code\u003e to require screen-recording permission (otherwise you’ll get \u003ccode\u003ePERMISSION_MISSING\u003c/code\u003e).\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003esystem.notify\u003c/code\u003e posts a user notification and fails if notifications are denied.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003ecanvas.*\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ecamera.*\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003escreen.record\u003c/code\u003e, and \u003ccode\u003elocation.get\u003c/code\u003e are also routed via \u003ccode\u003enode.invoke\u003c/code\u003e and follow TCC permission status.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eElevated bash (host permissions) is separate from macOS TCC:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eUse \u003ccode\u003e/elevated on|off\u003c/code\u003e to toggle per‑session elevated access when enabled + allowlisted.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eGateway persists the per‑session toggle via \u003ccode\u003esessions.patch\u003c/code\u003e (WS method) alongside \u003ccode\u003ethinkingLevel\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003everboseLevel\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003emodel\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003esendPolicy\u003c/code\u003e, and \u003ccode\u003egroupActivation\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eDetails: \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eNodes\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca href=\"https://docs.openclaw.ai/platforms/macos\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003emacOS app\u003c/a\u003e · \u003ca Teams/WebChat (group commands are owner-only):\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/status\u003c/code\u003e — compact session status (model + tokens, cost when available)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/new\u003c/code\u003e or \u003ccode\u003e/reset\u003c/code\u003e — reset the session\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/compact\u003c/code\u003e — compact session context (summary)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/think \u0026lt;level\u0026gt;\u003c/code\u003e — off|minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh (GPT-5.2 + Codex models only)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/verbose on|off\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/usage off|tokens|full\u003c/code\u003e — per-response usage footer\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/restart\u003c/code\u003e — restart the gateway (owner-only in groups)\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/activation harden read-tool overflow guards and sticky reply threading (#19508)

* fix(gateway): avoid premature agent.wait completion on transient errors

* fix(agent): preemptively guard tool results against context overflow

* fix: harden tool-result context guard and add message_id metadata

* fix: use importOriginal in session-key mock to include DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID

The run.skill-filter test was mocking ../../routing/session-key.js with only
buildAgentMainSessionKey and normalizeAgentId, but the module also exports
DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID which is required transitively by src/web/auth-store.ts.

Switch to importOriginal pattern so all real exports are preserved alongside
the mocked functions.

* pi-runner: guard accumulated tool-result overflow in transformContext

* PI runner: compact overflowing tool-result context

* Subagent: harden tool-result context recovery

* Enhance tool-result context handling by adding support for legacy tool outputs and improving character estimation for message truncation. This includes a new function to create legacy tool results and updates to existing functions to better manage context overflow scenarios.

* Enhance iMessage handling by adding reply tag support in send functions and tests. This includes modifications to prepend or rewrite reply tags based on provided replyToId, ensuring proper message formatting for replies.

* Enhance message delivery across multiple channels by implementing sticky reply context for chunked messages. This includes preserving reply references in Discord, Telegram, and iMessage, ensuring that follow-up messages maintain their intended reply targets. Additionally, improve handling of reply tags in system prompts and tests to support consistent reply behavior.

* Enhance read tool functionality by implementing auto-paging across chunks when no explicit limit is provided, scaling output budget based on model context window. Additionally, add tests for adaptive reading behavior and capped continuation guidance for large outputs. Update related functions to support these features.

* Refine tool-result context management by stripping oversized read-tool details payloads during compaction, ensuring repeated read calls do not bypass context limits. Introduce new utility functions for handling truncation content and enhance character estimation for tool results. Add tests to validate the removal of excessive details in context overflow scenarios.

* Refine message delivery logic in Matrix and Telegram by introducing a flag to track if a text chunk was sent. This ensures that replies are only marked as delivered when a text chunk has been successfully sent, improving the accuracy of reply handling in both channels.

dashboard with glassmorphism theme system

Add a full-featured gateway dashboard UI built on Lit web components.

Shell & plumbing:
- App shell with router, controllers, and dependency wiring
- Login gate, i18n keys, and base layout scaffolding

Styles & theming:
- Base styles, chat styles, and responsive layout CSS
- 6-theme glassmorphism system (Obsidian, Aurora, Solar, etc.)
- Glass card, glass panel, and glass input components
- Favicon logo in expanded sidebar header

Views & features:
- Overview with attention cards, event log, quick actions, and log tail
- Chat view with markdown rendering, tool-call collapse, and delete support
- Command palette with fuzzy search
- Agent overview with config display, slash commands, and sidebar filtering
- Session list navigation and agent selector

Privacy & polish:
- Redact toggle with stream-mode default
- Blur host/IP in Connected Instances with reveal toggle
- Sensitive config value masking with count badge
- Card accent borders, hover lift effects, and responsive grid" class="Link--secondary" href="/openclaw/openclaw/commit/3bbbe33a1b91c3cfe2327e2d5655c19c0b9fe3f8">UI: gateway dashboard with title="fix(docker): pin base images to SHA256 digests (#7734)

* fix(docker): pin base images to SHA256 digests for supply chain security

Pin all 9 Dockerfiles to immutable SHA256 digests to prevent supply chain
attacks where a compromised upstream image could be silently pulled into
production builds.

Also add Docker ecosystem to Dependabot configuration for automated
digest updates.

Images pinned:
- node:22-bookworm@sha256:cd7bcd2e7a1e6f72052feb023c7f6b722205d3fcab7bbcbd2d1bfdab10b1e935
- node:22-bookworm-slim@sha256:3cfe526ec8dd62013b8843e8e5d4877e297b886e5aace4a59fec25dc20736e45
- debian:bookworm-slim@sha256:98f4b71de414932439ac6ac690d7060df1f27161073c5036a7553723881bffbe
- ubuntu:24.04@sha256:cd1dba651b3080c3686ecf4e3c4220f026b521fb76978881737d24f200828b2b

Fixes #7731

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title="feat(podman): add optional Podman setup and documentation (#16273)

* feat(podman): add optional Podman setup and documentation

- Introduced `setup-podman.sh` for one-time host setup of OpenClaw in a rootless Podman environment, including user creation, image building, and launch script installation.
- Added `run-openclaw-podman.sh` for running the OpenClaw gateway as a Podman container.
- Created `openclaw.podman.env` for environment variable configuration.
- Updated documentation to include Podman installation instructions and a new dedicated Podman guide.
- Added a systemd Quadlet unit for managing the OpenClaw service as a user service.

* fix: harden Podman setup and docs (#16273) (thanks @DarwinsBuddy)

* style: format cli credentials

---------

title="feat(channels): add Synology Chat native channel (#23012)

* feat(channels): add Synology Chat native channel

Webhook-based integration with Synology NAS Chat (DSM 7+).
Supports outgoing webhooks, incoming messages, multi-account,
DM policies, rate limiting, and input sanitization.

- HMAC-based constant-time token validation
- Configurable SSL verification (allowInsecureSsl) for self-signed NAS certs
- 54 unit tests across 5 test suites
- Follows the same ChannelPlugin pattern as LINE/Discord/Telegram

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(synology-chat): add pairing, warnings, messaging, agent hints

- Enable media capability (file_url already supported by client)
- Add pairing.notifyApproval to message approved users
- Add security.collectWarnings for missing token/URL, insecure SSL, open DM policy
- Add messaging.normalizeTarget and targetResolver for user ID resolution
- Add directory stubs (self, listPeers, listGroups)
- Add agentPrompt.messageToolHints with Synology Chat formatting guide
- 63 tests (up from 54), all passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add prek pre-commit hooks and dependabot

Pre-commit hooks (via prek):
- Basic hygiene: trailing-whitespace, end-of-file-fixer, check-yaml, check-added-large-files, check-merge-conflict
- Security: detect-secrets, zizmor (GitHub Actions audit)
- Linting: shellcheck, actionlint, oxlint, swiftlint
- Formatting: oxfmt, swiftformat

Dependabot:
- npm and GitHub Actions ecosystems
- Grouped updates (production/development/actions)
- 7-day cooldown for supply chain protection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add prek install instruction to AGENTS.md

<p dir="auto"><strong>OpenClaw</strong> is a <em>personal AI assistant</em> you run on your own devices.
It answers you on the channels you already use (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord, Google Chat, Signal, iMessage, Microsoft Teams, WebChat), plus extension channels like BlueBubbles, Matrix, Zalo, and Zalo Personal. It can speak and listen on macOS/iOS/Android, and can render a live Canvas you control. The Gateway is just the control plane — the product is the assistant.</p>
<p dir="auto">If you want a personal, single-user assistant that feels local, fast, and always-on, this is it.</p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://openclaw.ai" rel="nofollow">Website</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai" rel="nofollow">Docs</a> · <a href="/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md">Vision</a> · <a href="https://deepwiki.com/openclaw/openclaw" rel="nofollow">DeepWiki</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/getting-started" rel="nofollow">Getting Started</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/install/updating" rel="nofollow">Updating</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/showcase" rel="nofollow">Showcase</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/faq" rel="nofollow">FAQ</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/wizard" rel="nofollow">Wizard</a> · <a href="https://github.com/openclaw/nix-openclaw">Nix</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/install/docker" rel="nofollow">Docker</a> · <a href="https://discord.gg/clawd" rel="nofollow">Discord</a></p>
<p dir="auto">Preferred setup: run the onboarding wizard (<code>openclaw onboard</code>) in your terminal.
The wizard guides you step by step through setting up the gateway, workspace, channels, and skills. The CLI wizard is the recommended path and works on <strong>macOS, Linux, and Windows (via WSL2; strongly recommended)</strong>.
Works with npm, pnpm, or bun.
New install? Start here: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/getting-started" rel="nofollow">Getting started</a></p>

<p dir="auto"><strong>Subscriptions (OAuth):</strong></p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong><a href="https://www.anthropic.com/" rel="nofollow">Anthropic</a></strong> (Claude Pro/Max)</li>

<p dir="auto">Model note: while any model is supported, I strongly recommend <strong>Anthropic Pro/Max (100/200) + Opus 4.6</strong> for long‑context strength and better prompt‑injection resistance. See <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/onboarding" rel="nofollow">Onboarding</a>.</p>

<li><strong>stable</strong>: tagged releases (<code>vYYYY.M.D</code> or <code>vYYYY.M.D-<patch></code>), npm dist-tag <code>latest</code>.</li>
<li><strong>beta</strong>: prerelease tags (<code>vYYYY.M.D-beta.N</code>), npm dist-tag <code>beta</code> (macOS app may be missing).</li>
<li><strong>dev</strong>: moving head of <code>main</code>, npm dist-tag <code>dev</code> (when published).</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Switch channels (git + npm): <code>openclaw update --channel stable|beta|dev</code>.
Details: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/install/development-channels" rel="nofollow">Development channels</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">From source (development)</h2><a id="user-content-from-source-development" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: From source (development)" href="#from-source-development"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Prefer <code>pnpm</code> for builds from source. Bun is optional for running TypeScript directly.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>git clone https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw.git
<span class="pl-c1">cd</span> openclaw

pnpm install
pnpm ui:build <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> auto-installs UI deps on first run</span>
pnpm build

pnpm openclaw onboard --install-daemon

<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Dev loop (auto-reload on TS changes)</span>
pnpm gateway:watch</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Security defaults (DM access)</h2><a id="user-content-security-defaults-dm-access" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Security defaults (DM access)" href="#security-defaults-dm-access"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">OpenClaw connects to real messaging surfaces. Treat inbound DMs as <strong>untrusted input</strong>.</p>
<p dir="auto">Full security guide: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/security" rel="nofollow">Security</a></p>
<p dir="auto">Default behavior on Telegram/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/Microsoft Teams/Discord/Google Chat/Slack:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>DM pairing</strong> (<code>dmPolicy="pairing"</code> / <code>channels.discord.dmPolicy="pairing"</code> / <code>channels.slack.dmPolicy="pairing"</code>; legacy: <code>channels.discord.dm.policy</code>, <code>channels.slack.dm.policy</code>): unknown senders receive a short pairing code and the bot does not process their message.</li>
<li>Approve with: <code>openclaw pairing approve <channel> <code></code> (then the sender is added to a local allowlist store).</li>
<li>Public inbound DMs require an explicit opt-in: set <code>dmPolicy="open"</code> and include <code>"*"</code> in the channel allowlist (<code>allowFrom</code> / <code>channels.discord.allowFrom</code> / <code>channels.slack.allowFrom</code>; legacy: <code>channels.discord.dm.allowFrom</code>, <code>channels.slack.dm.allowFrom</code>).</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Run <code>openclaw doctor</code> to surface risky/misconfigured DM policies.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Highlights</h2><a id="user-content-highlights" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Highlights" href="#highlights"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway" rel="nofollow">Local-first Gateway</a></strong> — single control plane for sessions, channels, tools, and events.</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels" rel="nofollow">Multi-channel inbox</a></strong> — WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord, Google Chat, Signal, BlueBubbles (iMessage), iMessage (legacy), Microsoft Teams, Matrix, Zalo, Zalo Personal, WebChat, macOS, iOS/Android.</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/configuration" rel="nofollow">Multi-agent routing</a></strong> — route inbound channels/accounts/peers to isolated agents (workspaces + per-agent sessions).</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes/voicewake" rel="nofollow">Voice Wake</a> + <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes/talk" rel="nofollow">Talk Mode</a></strong> — always-on speech for macOS/iOS/Android with ElevenLabs.</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/platforms/mac/canvas" rel="nofollow">Live Canvas</a></strong> — agent-driven visual workspace with <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/platforms/mac/canvas#canvas-a2ui" rel="nofollow">A2UI</a>.</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools" rel="nofollow">First-class tools</a></strong> — browser, canvas, nodes, cron, sessions, and Discord/Slack actions.</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/platforms/macos" rel="nofollow">Companion apps</a></strong> — macOS menu bar app + iOS/Android <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes" rel="nofollow">nodes</a>.</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/wizard" rel="nofollow">Onboarding</a> + <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/skills" rel="nofollow">skills</a></strong> — wizard-driven setup with bundled/managed/workspace skills.</li>
</ul>

<ul dir="auto">
<li><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway" rel="nofollow">Gateway WS control plane</a> with sessions, presence, config, cron, webhooks, <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/web" rel="nofollow">Control UI</a>, and <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/platforms/mac/canvas#canvas-a2ui" rel="nofollow">Canvas host</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/agent-send" rel="nofollow">CLI surface</a>: gateway, agent, send, <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/wizard" rel="nofollow">wizard</a>, and <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/doctor" rel="nofollow">doctor</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/agent" rel="nofollow">Pi agent runtime</a> in RPC mode with tool streaming and block streaming.</li>
<li><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/session" rel="nofollow">Session model</a>: <code>main</code> for direct chats, group isolation, activation modes, queue modes, reply-back. Group rules: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/groups" rel="nofollow">Groups</a>.</li>
<li><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes/images" rel="nofollow">Media pipeline</a>: images/audio/video, transcription hooks, size caps, temp file lifecycle. Audio details: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes/audio" rel="nofollow">Audio</a>.</li>
</ul>

<li><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes" rel="nofollow">macOS node mode</a>: system.run/notify + canvas/camera exposure.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Tools + automation</h3><a id="user-content-tools--automation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Tools + automation" href="#tools--automation"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<li><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/cron-jobs" rel="nofollow">Cron + wakeups</a>; <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/webhook" rel="nofollow">webhooks</a>; <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/automation/gmail-pubsub" rel="nofollow">Gmail Pub/Sub</a>.</li>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Key subsystems</h2><a id="user-content-key-subsystems" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Key subsystems" href="#key-subsystems"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/architecture" rel="nofollow">Gateway WebSocket network</a></strong> — single WS control plane for clients, tools, and events (plus ops: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway" rel="nofollow">Gateway runbook</a>).</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/tailscale" rel="nofollow">Tailscale exposure</a></strong> — Serve/Funnel for the Gateway dashboard + WS (remote access: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/remote" rel="nofollow">Remote</a>).</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/browser" rel="nofollow">Browser control</a></strong> — openclaw‑managed Chrome/Chromium with CDP control.</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/platforms/mac/canvas" rel="nofollow">Canvas + A2UI</a></strong> — agent‑driven visual workspace (A2UI host: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/platforms/mac/canvas#canvas-a2ui" rel="nofollow">Canvas/A2UI</a>).</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes/voicewake" rel="nofollow">Voice Wake</a> + <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes/talk" rel="nofollow">Talk Mode</a></strong> — always‑on speech and continuous conversation.</li>
<li><strong><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes" rel="nofollow">Nodes</a></strong> — Canvas, camera snap/clip, screen record, <code>location.get</code>, notifications, plus macOS‑only <code>system.run</code>/<code>system.notify</code>.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Tailscale access (Gateway dashboard)</h2><a id="user-content-tailscale-access-gateway-dashboard" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Tailscale access (Gateway dashboard)" href="#tailscale-access-gateway-dashboard"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">OpenClaw can auto-configure Tailscale <strong>Serve</strong> (tailnet-only) or <strong>Funnel</strong> (public) while the Gateway stays bound to loopback. Configure <code>gateway.tailscale.mode</code>:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>off</code>: no Tailscale automation (default).</li>
<li><code>serve</code>: tailnet-only HTTPS via <code>tailscale serve</code> (uses Tailscale identity headers by default).</li>
<li><code>funnel</code>: public HTTPS via <code>tailscale funnel</code> (requires shared password auth).</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Notes:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>gateway.bind</code> must stay <code>loopback</code> when Serve/Funnel is enabled (OpenClaw enforces this).</li>
<li>Serve can be forced to require a password by setting <code>gateway.auth.mode: "password"</code> or <code>gateway.auth.allowTailscale: false</code>.</li>
<li>Funnel refuses to start unless <code>gateway.auth.mode: "password"</code> is set.</li>
<li>Optional: <code>gateway.tailscale.resetOnExit</code> to undo Serve/Funnel on shutdown.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Details: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/tailscale" rel="nofollow">Tailscale guide</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/web" rel="nofollow">Web surfaces</a></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Remote Gateway (Linux is great)</h2><a id="user-content-remote-gateway-linux-is-great" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Remote Gateway (Linux is great)" href="#remote-gateway-linux-is-great"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">It’s perfectly fine to run the Gateway on a small Linux instance. Clients (macOS app, CLI, WebChat) can connect over <strong>Tailscale Serve/Funnel</strong> or <strong>SSH tunnels</strong>, and you can still pair device nodes (macOS/iOS/Android) to execute device‑local actions when needed.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Gateway host</strong> runs the exec tool and channel connections by default.</li>
<li><strong>Device nodes</strong> run device‑local actions (<code>system.run</code>, camera, screen recording, notifications) via <code>node.invoke</code>.
In short: exec runs where the Gateway lives; device actions run where the device lives.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Details: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/remote" rel="nofollow">Remote access</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes" rel="nofollow">Nodes</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/security" rel="nofollow">Security</a></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">macOS permissions via the Gateway protocol</h2><a id="user-content-macos-permissions-via-the-gateway-protocol" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: macOS permissions via the Gateway protocol" href="#macos-permissions-via-the-gateway-protocol"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The macOS app can run in <strong>node mode</strong> and advertises its capabilities + permission map over the Gateway WebSocket (<code>node.list</code> / <code>node.describe</code>). Clients can then execute local actions via <code>node.invoke</code>:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>system.run</code> runs a local command and returns stdout/stderr/exit code; set <code>needsScreenRecording: true</code> to require screen-recording permission (otherwise you’ll get <code>PERMISSION_MISSING</code>).</li>
<li><code>system.notify</code> posts a user notification and fails if notifications are denied.</li>
<li><code>canvas.*</code>, <code>camera.*</code>, <code>screen.record</code>, and <code>location.get</code> are also routed via <code>node.invoke</code> and follow TCC permission status.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Elevated bash (host permissions) is separate from macOS TCC:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Use <code>/elevated on|off</code> to toggle per‑session elevated access when enabled + allowlisted.</li>
<li>Gateway persists the per‑session toggle via <code>sessions.patch</code> (WS method) alongside <code>thinkingLevel</code>, <code>verboseLevel</code>, <code>model</code>, <code>sendPolicy</code>, and <code>groupActivation</code>.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Details: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/nodes" rel="nofollow">Nodes</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/platforms/macos" rel="nofollow">macOS app</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/architecture" rel="nofollow">Gateway protocol</a></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Agent to Agent (sessions_* tools)</h2><a id="user-content-agent-to-agent-sessions_-tools" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Agent to Agent (sessions_* tools)" href="#agent-to-agent-sessions_-tools"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Use these to coordinate work across sessions without jumping between chat surfaces.</li>
<li><code>sessions_list</code> — discover active sessions (agents) and their metadata.</li>
<li><code>sessions_history</code> — fetch transcript logs for a session.</li>
<li><code>sessions_send</code> — message another session; optional reply‑back ping‑pong + announce step (<code>REPLY_SKIP</code>, <code>ANNOUNCE_SKIP</code>).</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Details: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/session-tool" rel="nofollow">Session tools</a></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Skills registry (ClawHub)</h2><a id="user-content-skills-registry-clawhub" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Skills registry (ClawHub)" href="#skills-registry-clawhub"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">ClawHub is a minimal skill registry. With ClawHub enabled, the agent can search for skills automatically and pull in new ones as needed.</p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://clawhub.com" rel="nofollow">ClawHub</a></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Chat commands</h2><a id="user-content-chat-commands" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Chat commands" href="#chat-commands"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Send these in WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Google Chat/Microsoft Teams/WebChat (group commands are owner-only):</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><code>/status</code> — compact session status (model + tokens, cost when available)</li>
<li><code>/new</code> or <code>/reset</code> — reset the session</li>
<li><code>/compact</code> — compact session context (summary)</li>
<li><code>/think <level></code> — off|minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh (GPT-5.2 + Codex models only)</li>
<li><code>/verbose on|off</code></li>
<li><code>/usage off|tokens|full</code> — per-response usage footer</li>
<li><code>/restart</code> — restart the gateway (owner-only in groups)</li>
<li><code>/activation mention|always</code> — group activation toggle (groups only)</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Apps (optional)</h2><a id="user-content-apps-optional" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Apps (optional)" href="#apps-optional"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The Gateway alone delivers a great experience. All apps are optional and add extra features.</p>
<p dir="auto">If you plan to build/run companion apps, follow the platform runbooks below.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">macOS (OpenClaw.app) (optional)</h3><a id="user-content-macos-openclawapp-optional" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: macOS (OpenClaw.app) (optional)" href="#macos-openclawapp-optional"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Menu bar control for the Gateway and health.</li>
<li>Voice Wake + push-to-talk overlay.</li>
<li>WebChat + debug tools.</li>
<li>Remote gateway control over SSH.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Note: signed builds required for macOS permissions to stick across rebuilds (see <code>docs/mac/permissions.md</code>).</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Agent workspace + skills</h2><a id="user-content-agent-workspace--skills" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Agent workspace + skills" href="#agent-workspace--skills"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Workspace root: <code>~/.openclaw/workspace</code> (configurable via <code>agents.defaults.workspace</code>).</li>
<li>Injected prompt files: <code>AGENTS.md</code>, <code>SOUL.md</code>, <code>TOOLS.md</code>.</li>
<li>Skills: <code>~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md</code>.</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Security model (important)</h2><a id="user-content-security-model-important" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Security model (important)" href="#security-model-important"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Default:</strong> tools run on the host for the <strong>main</strong> session, so the agent has full access when it’s just you.</li>
<li><strong>Group/channel safety:</strong> set <code>agents.defaults.sandbox.mode: "non-main"</code> to run <strong>non‑main sessions</strong> (groups/channels) inside per‑session Docker sandboxes; bash then runs in Docker for those sessions.</li>
<li><strong>Sandbox defaults:</strong> allowlist <code>bash</code>, <code>process</code>, <code>read</code>, <code>write</code>, <code>edit</code>, <code>sessions_list</code>, <code>sessions_history</code>, <code>sessions_send</code>, <code>sessions_spawn</code>; denylist <code>browser</code>, <code>canvas</code>, <code>nodes</code>, <code>cron</code>, <code>discord</code>, <code>gateway</code>.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">Details: <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/security" rel="nofollow">Security guide</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/install/docker" rel="nofollow">Docker + sandboxing</a> · <a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/configuration" rel="nofollow">Sandbox config</a></p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto"><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/whatsapp" rel="nofollow">WhatsApp</a></h3><a id="user-content-whatsapp" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: WhatsApp" href="#whatsapp"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Link the device: <code>pnpm openclaw channels login</code> (stores creds in <code>~/.openclaw/credentials</code>).</li>
<li>Allowlist who can talk to the assistant via <code>channels.whatsapp.allowFrom</code>.</li>
<li>If <code>channels.whatsapp.groups</code> is set, it becomes a group allowlist; include <code>"*"</code> to allow all.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto"><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/telegram" rel="nofollow">Telegram</a></h3><a id="user-content-telegram" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Telegram" href="#telegram"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Set <code>TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN</code> or <code>channels.telegram.botToken</code> (env wins).</li>
<li>Optional: set <code>channels.telegram.groups</code> (with <code>channels.telegram.groups."*".requireMention</code>); when set, it is a group allowlist (include <code>"*"</code> to allow all). Also <code>channels.telegram.allowFrom</code> or <code>channels.telegram.webhookUrl</code> + <code>channels.telegram.webhookSecret</code> as needed.</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto"><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/slack" rel="nofollow">Slack</a></h3><a id="user-content-slack" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Slack" href="#slack"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Set <code>SLACK_BOT_TOKEN</code> + <code>SLACK_APP_TOKEN</code> (or <code>channels.slack.botToken</code> + <code>channels.slack.appToken</code>).</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto"><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord" rel="nofollow">Discord</a></h3><a id="user-content-discord" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Discord" href="#discord"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Set <code>DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN</code> or <code>channels.discord.token</code> (env wins).</li>
<li>Optional: set <code>commands.native</code>, <code>commands.text</code>, or <code>commands.useAccessGroups</code>, plus <code>channels.discord.allowFrom</code>, <code>channels.discord.guilds</code>, or <code>channels.discord.mediaMaxMb</code> as needed.</li>
</ul>

<ul dir="auto">
<li>Requires <code>signal-cli</code> and a <code>channels.signal</code> config section.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto"><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/bluebubbles" rel="nofollow">BlueBubbles (iMessage)</a></h3><a id="user-content-bluebubbles-imessage" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: BlueBubbles (iMessage)" href="#bluebubbles-imessage"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Recommended</strong> iMessage integration.</li>
<li>Configure <code>channels.bluebubbles.serverUrl</code> + <code>channels.bluebubbles.password</code> and a webhook (<code>channels.bluebubbles.webhookPath</code>).</li>
<li>The BlueBubbles server runs on macOS; the Gateway can run on macOS or elsewhere.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto"><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/imessage" rel="nofollow">iMessage (legacy)</a></h3><a id="user-content-imessage-legacy" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: iMessage (legacy)" href="#imessage-legacy"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Legacy macOS-only integration via <code>imsg</code> (Messages must be signed in).</li>
<li>If <code>channels.imessage.groups</code> is set, it becomes a group allowlist; include <code>"*"</code> to allow all.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto"><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/msteams" rel="nofollow">Microsoft Teams</a></h3><a id="user-content-microsoft-teams" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Microsoft Teams" href="#microsoft-teams"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Configure a Teams app + Bot Framework, then add a <code>msteams</code> config section.</li>
<li>Allowlist who can talk via <code>msteams.allowFrom</code>; group access via <code>msteams.groupAllowFrom</code> or <code>msteams.groupPolicy: "open"</code>.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto"><a href="https://docs.openclaw.ai/web/webchat" rel="nofollow">WebChat</a></h3><a id="user-content-webchat" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: WebChat" href="#webchat"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Uses the Gateway WebSocket; no separate WebChat port/config.</li>
</ul>
